title: SwiftLib Site Adapter
description: Machine-readable contract for Resources/adapters/*.json. AI repair agents MUST stay within this schema: the runtime has a fixed set of transforms / postProcess / kinds, and invented names silently degrade to identity functions. If you need a capability that isn't here, the fix must be in Swift (runtime extension) first, not in adapter JSON.

id.description: Unique adapter identifier, lowercase kebab-case. File name must match: <id>.json.

schemaVersion.description: Bump on every schema drift fix so we can trace who changed what.

routes.description: Route name → definition. Conventional names: search, byDoi, byTitle, byTitleMatch, byIsbn, byPmid, byArxiv, abstractByDoi, detail. Swift callers hardcode these names.

canary.description: Live-URL verification fixtures. ≥1 per adapter is STRONGLY recommended; canary failures are the primary drift signal.

url.description: URL template. {placeholder} substrings are filled from caller context via expandURL(_:context:). Keys ending in Url/URL are substituted verbatim (no percent-encoding); other keys are URL-query-encoded. Empty &key= query params get auto-scrubbed.

kind.description: Transport executor. `http` uses URLSession + SiteAdapterRuntime. `webView` loads the URL in a hidden WKWebView (reusing existing browser cookies via WebSessionBroker) and returns the rendered page source for extraction. Both are implemented; default is `http` for backward compatibility.

requiresAuthenticatedSession.description: Meaningful only for kind=webView. Unused today.

kind.description: ONLY json and html are implemented. xml is NOT yet supported — do not emit it.

itemsPath.description: Dot-path to the array/object to iterate. '$' means root. Supports {placeholder} expansion against caller context (required for Open Library's dynamic ISBN root). Defaults to '$'.

paths.description: JSON-mode: ordered candidate paths; first non-empty wins. Supported syntax: $ | $.foo | foo.bar | foo[0] | foo[*] | foo[*].bar (array flattens to string array, joined by `separator`).

strategies.description: HTML-mode: ordered extraction strategies; first match wins. Only regex strategy implemented.

template.description: Computed-mode: render {json.path} placeholders against the current item. Example: '{biblio.first_page}-{biblio.last_page}'.

elideIfMissing.description: Only for template mode: if ANY listed path is absent in the item, the whole field resolves to null (prevents half-rendered strings like '101-').

separator.description: Join separator when a resolved path yields an array. Defaults to '|'.

transform.description: STRING-level post-processor applied after stringify. These are the ONLY valid values. Inventing names silently returns identity. To add a transform, edit SiteAdapterRuntime.applyTransform() in Swift FIRST.

postProcess.description: RAW-VALUE post-processor applied to the Any resolved from paths, before stringify. These are the ONLY valid values. Currently only OpenAlex's inverted-index abstract is supported. To add, edit SiteAdapterRuntime.applyPostProcess() in Swift FIRST.

pattern.description: NSRegularExpression with .dotMatchesLineSeparators + .caseInsensitive options preset. MUST anchor via nearby HTML landmarks (class=, id=, meta property=) to avoid false matches in long bodies.

stripTags.description: When true, strips <...> tags and collapses whitespace before returning.

route.description: Explicit route name to exercise. REQUIRED when the adapter defines multiple search-like routes (e.g. byDoi AND byTitle).

searchQuery.description: Shorthand: populates {query}, {doi}, {title}, {isbn}, {pmid}, {arxivId} all at once. Good for single-placeholder URLs. Prefer explicit `context` for clarity.

context.description: Explicit placeholder → value map passed to BOTH expandURL AND extractJSON. Required for routes whose URL or itemsPath uses non-`{query}` placeholders.

expectSearch.description: Expected subset of the extracted first row. Keys must be field names declared in the route's `extract.fields`. Values are STRING matches (adapter rows are always stringified).

expectDetail.description: Expected subset of the detail route output, when subjectUrl is set.
